mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near the zero lower bound (0.07% as of Feb-2014), sustained near-zero policy rates compress the equity risk premium discount factor, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 prices over a full quarter as investors reach for yield in risk assets.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.07% for multiple consecutive months as of 2014-02-01
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during near-zero FEDFUNDS periods are not significantly higher than the unconditional historical mean (t-test p > 0.05), or rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS month-over-month gains accelerate and cross above a 150k threshold following a period of sub-trend growth, it signals labor market re-acceleration that raises earnings expectations, driving S&P 500 forward returns higher over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly net change exceeds +150,000 after two prior months below +150,000
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS revised_value=137712.0 thousand as of vintage 2014-02-01
failure_condition: Proportion of positive 20-day forward returns following triggered PAYEMS acceleration is not statistically greater than baseline 65.5% positive rate, or rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) reflects broad-based consumer demand strength that is not captured by either series alone; when both rise simultaneously month-over-month, forward-looking earnings revisions turn positive and S&P 500 returns rise over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth > 0.3% AND HOUST MoM growth > 2% in the same month
regime_hint: expansion phase with strengthening consumer and housing demand
failure_condition: Joint-trigger 20-day forward return distribution is not significantly different from single-series-only trigger cases (ANOVA p > 0.05), indicating no true interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPIAUCSL print that surprises meaningfully above the recent trailing 6-month average signals inflation overshoot risk, raising expectations of future policy tightening and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL MoM change exceeds trailing 6-month average MoM change by more than 0.2 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL revised_value=235.288 vs vintage_value=234.933 as of 2014-01-01
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following a CPI upside surprise trigger are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in INDPRO (industrial production index) reflects expanding real economic output and corporate revenue capacity, which supports higher forward corporate earnings and drives a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 prices over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during INDPRO uptrend periods are indistinguishable from unconditional baseline returns (t-test p > 0.05), or rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a rise in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points from its 12-month trailing low signals the early stage of labor market deterioration analogous to a Sahm-rule trigger, foreshadowing earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp above its trailing 12-month minimum
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=6.7% as of 2014-02-01, still declining from cycle highs
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with rising unemployment turning point
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following the UNRATE trigger are not significantly negative relative to baseline, or the trigger fails to precede any observed drawdown greater than 5% within 3 months in more than 50% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in HOUST exceeding 5% signals weakening forward housing demand and consumer confidence, a leading indicator of broader demand slowdown, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns after a HOUST decline trigger are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or triggered instances show positive returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS remains near zero while UNRATE simultaneously falls below 6.5% (the Fed's previously stated forward guidance threshold), markets anticipate imminent policy liftoff, causing a short-term repricing of rate-sensitive equities and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as tightening expectations are pulled forward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=6.7% as of 2014-02-01 approaching the 6.5% forward-guidance threshold
regime_hint: zero lower bound liftoff anticipation regime
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during joint trigger periods are not significantly negative relative to baseline, or the joint-trigger effect is statistically indistinguishable from the UNRATE-only single-series effect.
